findings.field: Professional Summary; TikTok Experience bullets; DiDi Experience bullets
findings.issue: Several modifier words are bolded (`team-maintained`, `existing`, `intern-owned`, `team-owned`, `shared`). The template wants bold emphasis reserved for technical nouns, scope numbers, or section headers, not adjectival qualifiers.
findings.fix: Unbold qualifier words and keep bolding for role headers, technologies, scope metrics, and other signal-bearing nouns only.

findings.issue: The opening framing is transition-first (`Backend Engineer Transitioning from Analytics`), which weakens the first-screen role signal for a backend opening.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first sentence to lead with a direct target-role framing, e.g. `Security/Backend Engineer:` or `Backend Engineer:`, then keep the analytics background as supporting context.

findings.field: TikTok · Security | Software Engineer Intern | Jun 2025 – Dec 2025
findings.issue: The latest/relevant experience is dominated by percentage lifts (`28%`, `19%`, `31%`, `18%`) without any concrete Tier 1 scope anchors such as request volume, analyst count, event throughput, release cadence, or team size.
findings.fix: Add 1-2 concrete scope signals to the lead bullets or project bullets, such as number of analysts supported, requests/day, Kafka event volume, release frequency, or SLA/latency baseline.

findings.field: Temu · R&D | Data Analyst | Jun 2021 – Feb 2022
findings.issue: This early experience is almost entirely expressed as time saved or percentage improvement, with no Tier 1 scale signal to anchor the scope of the work.
findings.fix: Add a concrete scope anchor to one bullet, such as reporting audience size, query volume, batch frequency, data scale, or the size of the product/research surface.

revision_priority: [MUST] Rewrite the Summary opening to be role-first rather than transition-first, so the resume reads as an immediately credible backend/security profile.
[MUST] Add concrete Tier 1 scope anchors to the TikTok and Temu bullets to avoid a resume that is mostly percentage deltas without scale.
[SHOULD] Remove bolding from modifier words like `team-maintained`, `existing`, and `shared`; keep emphasis on technologies and scope signals.
raw_json.revision_instructions: Rewrite the first Summary sentence to lead with a direct backend/security role framing, not a transition narrative. Add 1-2 concrete scope anchors to the TikTok and Temu bullets or project bullets (requests/day, analysts supported, Kafka event volume, release cadence, SLA, or team size). Then reduce bolding on modifier words and keep emphasis on technologies, scope, and metrics.
